channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Afghan family welcomes charges against Ben Roberts-Smith over alleged 2009 killings in Uruzgan
item.description: Former Australian soldier and Victoria Cross recipient Ben Roberts-Smith has been charged with multiple counts of war crimes, including the alleged murders of unarmed civilians during his service in A...

item.title: Tanzania expands mobile and internet coverage aiming for full nationwide connectivity by 2030
item.description: Tanzania's communications sector don show serious growth in the past four years as the government dey push major infrastructure projects. Between 2021 and December 2025, access to mobile networks and ...

item.title: Artemis II Crew Returns to Earth with Pacific Ocean Splashdown After Historic Moon Mission
item.description: NASA's Artemis II mission has reached its conclusion as the four-member crew prepared to return to Earth following a historic 10-day journey around the Moon. The Orion spacecraft is scheduled to splas...

item.title: Ongoing water disruptions at Sefako Makgatho University affect student hygiene and daily routines
item.description: Students at Sefako Makgatho Health Sciences University have been facing ongoing water supply disruptions, significantly impacting daily life on campus. Intermittent water availability, caused by press...

item.title: Sam Ryder calls for kinder talent shows that prioritise contestant welfare
item.description: Sam Ryder, the UK representative for Eurovision 2022, has voiced criticism of past talent competitions, describing them as 'toxic' and warning against programmes that treat participants merely as comm...

item.title: Christine Otieno Hosts Gospel Concert to Support the Less Fortunate
item.description: Veteran Luo gospel musician Christine Otieno is organizing a major Christian concert aimed at bringing together gospel artistes from Kenya and East Africa for a time of worship and charity. The concer...

item.title: How to recover money sent to the wrong recipient in online transfers in the Philippines
item.description: In the Philippines, accidentally sending money to the wrong person through online transfers or e-wallets like GCash is a common mistake, especially when manually entering an 11-digit mobile number. Th...

item.title: Tanzanian and Moroccan Ambassadors Meet in Jakarta to Boost African Diplomatic Cooperation in Indonesia
item.description: On April 9, 2026, in Jakarta, the Tanzanian Ambassador to Indonesia and Dean of African Ambassadors, Macocha Moshe Tembele, held important talks with the newly appointed Ambassador of the Kingdom of M...

item.title: How Technology Can Help Your Family Stay Prepared for Emergencies
item.description: Emergencies can happen unexpectedly, whether due to natural disasters, accidents, medical issues, or financial crises. Being prepared as a family goes beyond just having a plan; it requires the right ...

item.title: Assessing New Zealand’s Potential for Marine Renewable Energy
item.description: New Zealand has substantial untapped potential for marine energy, owing to its location and ocean conditions. The country already relies heavily on hydro, geothermal, and wind energy, but offshore wav...

item.title: Retired Woman in Rajkot, Gujarat Duped of Rs 78 Lakh by Cyber Fraudsters Posing as CBI Officers in Digital Arrest Scam
item.description: A retired woman residing in Rajkot, Gujarat, became the victim of a sophisticated cyber scam where fraudsters kept her under 'digital arrest' for ten full days, ultimately cheating her of her life sav...

item.title: Ferroalloys-Eskom electricity tariff negotiations continue with 12,000 jobs at risk
item.description: Negotiations between Glencore's ferroalloys unit and Eskom over a proposed 62 cents per kilowatt-hour electricity tariff for the South African ferrochrome industry have dragged on without resolution, ...

item.title: Kitutu South Residents Demand Clarity Over Boundary Dispute With Nyaribari Chache
item.description: Tensions have flared up between Kitutu Chache South and Nyaribari Chache constituencies following protests by locals over what they claim is a violation of their constituency boundary. Residents of Ki...

item.title: FIFA Releases 170 Officials for 2026 World Cup with No Nigerian Included
item.description: FIFA don release the full list of 170 match officials wey go handle the 2026 World Cup wey go take place for United States, Mexico and Canada. The list get 52 centre referees, 88 assistant referees an...

item.title: Podcast examines calls to invoke the 25th Amendment against President Trump
item.description: In this episode of Politics Weekly America, host Jonathan Freedland discusses with the Guardian’s Washington bureau chief David Smith the recent calls from both the political left and right to remove ...

item.title: Repeated Attacks Reported Near Iran's Bushehr Nuclear Facility
item.description: Iranian officials have reported that the area surrounding the Bushehr nuclear power plant in southern Iran has been attacked for the fourth time amid ongoing regional conflict. The attack resulted in ...

item.title: Liquid C2 and Google Cloud open innovation hub in South Africa to boost AI skills and technology across Africa
item.description: Liquid C2, one company wey belong to Cassava Technologies, don open the first Partner Experience Centre for Africa wey dey powered by Google Cloud. The new centre dey Johannesburg, South Africa, and e...

item.title: Alberta's Separatist Movement Faces Legal Challenges Over Signature Petition
item.description: Alberta's separatist movement, led by Mitch Sylvestre and the group Stay Free Alberta, reached the goal of 178,000 signatures needed to trigger a referendum on the province’s independence. The deadlin...

item.title: Sikh pilgrims from India arrive in Pakistan via Wagah border for Baisakhi festival celebrations
item.description: Pakistan has welcomed the first batch of Sikh pilgrims from India who crossed the Wagah border on Friday to take part in the Baisakhi festival. The government issued visas to around 2,800 pilgrims for...

item.title: PNPA investigates hazing incident injuring 22 cadets
item.description: A recent hazing incident at the Philippine National Police Academy (PNPA) has left at least 22 4th Class cadets injured at the hands of three 3rd Class cadets. The PNP confirmed that the incident occu...
